input.title: Travel Booking Platform
input.description: Travel Booking Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, Elasticsearch for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Flight Search
requirements: Search flights by origin, destination, dates, passengers
Filter by airline, stops, departure time, price range
Sort by price, duration, departure time
Show baggage and cancellation policy per result

FR-02.title: Hotel Search
requirements: Search hotels by destination and dates
Filter by star rating, amenities, price

FR-03.title: Flight Booking
requirements: Select flight and enter passenger details

FR-06.title: Car Rental
requirements: Search available vehicles by location and dates
Filter by vehicle type, transmission, and price

FR-08.title: Visa and Document Checklist
requirements: Visa requirements by nationality and destination

FR-14.title: Travel Alerts
requirements: Price drop alerts for saved searches

FR-21.title: Recommendation Engine
requirements: Based on travel history and preferences

FR-32.title: Social Sharing
requirements: Share trip plans on social media

FR-33.title: Waitlist and Price Watch
requirements: Notification on availability or price change

FR-01.title: Plan Flight Search Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for flight search and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Search flights by origin, destination, dates, passengers; Filter by airline, stops, departure time, price range; Sort by price, duration, departure time.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for flight search is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for flight search are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for flight search can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Flight Search Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for flight search on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Search flights by origin, destination, dates, passengers; Filter by airline, stops, departure time, price range; Sort by price, duration, departure time.
acceptance_criteria: Core flight search business rules are implemented for Travel Booking Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for flight search
API/service behavior for flight search is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Flight Search Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for flight search with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The flight search user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for flight search behave correctly
The flight search UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Write Flight Search Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for flight search. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for flight search are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for flight search are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical flight search scenarios
